titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Collection of material relating to World War I
Collection of material relating to World War I
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai and Leigh Golden

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 05:27

did.unittitle: Collection of material relating to World War I

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 13 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

persname.part: Hankey, Maurice Pascal Alers Hankey, Baron, 1877-1963

did.unittitle: Diaries and sketchbooks

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by author's last name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains diaries as well as field message books, log books and sketchbooks. Material is primarily created by British service members during the war years while serving in Belgium, Egypt, France, Palestine, and other locations.

did.unittitle: Allen, (Sergeant) B. Diary. Being the Record of Scenes and Events in connection with the Campaign of the 2/5th Battalion Durham Light Infantry in Macedonia, Bulgaria and Armenia
Anonymous diary. "Diary of Great War sailor aboard HMS Vengeance"
Anonymous diary."Leo's Account of the 1914 War"
Barnes, (Sapper) John George. Diary of Great War Soldier. Sapper John George Barnes, 2nd Division, 5th Brigade, 2nd signallers company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Sapper Barnes' medals: Victory Medal (UK & Commonwealth), British War Medal and Order of the British Empire (MBE).

did.unittitle: Beatson, (Corporal) James Nichol. Diary including the Second Battle of Ypres
Body, (Major) Edward Upton. Diary of Great War Soldier Major Edward Upton Body MC, 130th Battery XL R.F.A
Bruce, (Lieutenant) Ralph S. Field Message book, D. Company, 7th Northumberland Fusiliers
Budenteig, (Captain) C.J. Field Message Book, 520th (London) Field Co Royal Engineers
Costobadie (Lieutenant) H.P. Diary of the 1914 Campaign including the Retreat from Mons
German Navy (Kaiserliche Marine). U-boat Log Book. U-53 under the command of Kapitänleutnant Otto von Schrader

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kapitänleutnant Otto von Schrader commanded U-53, a Type U 51 ocean-going diesel-powered torpedo attack boat, from 18 Aug 1918 to 29 Nov 1918.

did.unittitle: Gilbert, (Private) H.R. Sketchbook. Palestine and Egypt
Gray, Arthur. Diary in France, 8th Battalion Sherwood Foresters
Hargreaves, (Lieutenant) L.R. Typed Diary of Great War Soldier. Lieutenant Hargreaves Irish Guards
Henderson, (Sapper) Alfred. Diaries
Hill, (Sergeant) Arthur. Eleven diaries. First hand account of the Battle of Jutland
Hill, (Sergeant) Arthur. Eleven diaries. First hand account of the Battle of Jutland
Howard (Private). Diary including entries on the fall of Jerusalem
Hutchinson, (Private) Walter. Diary, including eyewitness account of the first day of the Battle of the Somme

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Private Hutchinson's Military Medal (MM), the decoration's ribbon, and two photographs of Hutchinson in uniform. Also included is a 9 carat gold half-hunter pocket watch, inscribed on the inside back with: "The Great War, Presented to Walter Hutchinson by the Conisborough Heroes Committee in recognition of his winning the M.M., Oct 5, 1917."

did.unittitle: Jefferies, (Gunner) Alfred. Diary of Great War Soldier
Jefferies, (Gunner) Alfred. Medals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Gunner Jefferies' Victory Medal (UK & Commonwealth) and British War Medal.

did.unittitle: Meeks, (Private) Ernest. Y.M.C.A. pocket New Testament bible
Meeks, (Private) Ernest. Diary fragment
Meeks, (Private) Ernest. Copy of extracts from 1916-1918 War diaries
Meeks, (Private) Ernest. Pocket Diary
Melmer, Walther. German Diary
Merritt, J. Field Message Book, 193 Siege Battery Royal Field Artillery
Middlemas, Harry P. Diary of Great War Wireless Operator
Moore, (Staff Sergeant) James Diaries.
Nixon, (Private) S.J. Diary of Great War Soldier Private S.J. Nixon of the 51st Notts and Derby Regiment
Pease, Mary C. Diary relating to Mary C. Pease (Voluntary Aid Detachment)
Peto, (Sergeant) John. Field Message Book. Army Book 152
Read, (Sergeant) H.S. Field Message Book, 151 Company ASC (Army Service Corps), Regiment No. S4 / 064694
Roberts, (Lance Corporal) Herbert papers
Rutherford, (Captain) Denis. Field Message Book, 18th Lancashire Fusiliers
Shields, (Private) Hugh J.S. War Diary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published diary of Hugh J.S. Shields, printed by J.C. and A.T. Sawtell, Yeovil Road, Sherborne.

did.unittitle: Smith, (Captain) Sydney Philip papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Captain Sydney Philip Smith died on 6 April 1918 while flying a Sopwith Camel, North East of Villers Bretonneux. He was the 76th victim of Manfred Albrecht Freiherr von Richthofen (2 May 1892 – 21 April 1918), the Red Baron.

Contains three volumes which include Captain Smith's flying log-books, correspondence to his parents, military papers, assorted wartime photographs and memorial documents, including a letter of condolence from Buckingham Palace.

did.unittitle: Todd, (Lieutenant) James Dowell. Archive of manuscript material and ephemera, 13th Battalion, Prince of Wales Own West Yorkshire Regiment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Lieutenant Todd's diaries, his regimental lapel badges with a medal for the Burma campaign, correspondence, numerous photographs, Lieutenant Todd's campaign medals (which include his Victory Medal and British War Medal, his swagger stick, a clipping of Todd's obituary and various other material.

did.unittitle: Tooley, (Sergeant) Thomas Edward. Pocket New Testament and Medals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Sergeant Tooley's medals: Victory Medal with Oak Leaf (UK & Commonwealth), British War Medal (in bronze), and 1914-15 Star.

did.unittitle: Weissenborn, Hellmuth. Sketchbook of the Eastern Front
Correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by the author's last name, or by correspondence title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters and postcards from various correspondents during and after the war years. Correspondents includes members of the British Army, Royal Navy, and other British government positions. Material related to the correspondents, such as photographs, clippings, and other material is included.

did.unittitle: Campbell, (Major) A. A Collection of papers, 2/3rd Scottish Horse
Franks, (Lance Corporal) Leslie B.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a dog tag, hospital postcard, and other correspondence.

unittitle.emph: Handwritten signals intercepted by wireless telegraphy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains transcription of signals intercepted between the Commander-in Chief of the Grand Fleet and the German High Seas Fleet (Hochseeflotte) Command, regarding the surrender of the latter.

did.unittitle: HM Government. Communiqués from the start of the Great War including German cavalry movement on the Belgian Frontier
Letter Case, London Rifle Brigade,"L.R.B. France"
Propoganda Postcards
Sanderson, (Captain) J.V. Letter to his wife., Royal Field Artillery
Short, (Lieutenant) Leslie. Archive of letters written mainly from the front
Sproule, (Major) Harry Hutton, OC. Nine letters to Mrs. Ashe in London from Western Front and Mesopotamia, "C" Squadron 4th Cavalry, Indian Army
Temperley, (Captain) C.E. Thirty four letters to Mumpty
Thornton, (Lieutenant) George. Fifteen letters detailing Lt. Thornton's death, Guards 16th Heavy Brigade
Tooley, (Sergeant) Thomas Edward. Letter to the BBC
Scrapbooks and photographs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by album creator or title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material in this series includes scrapbooks, photographs, postcard collections and autograph albums. Content includes photographs of servicepeople, aerial photographs, and signatures from British Colonels, Captains, Privates, and Seargeants.

did.unittitle: [Anonymous]. A Great War Autograph Book
Association of Former Members of the Bavarian Field Artillery No. 5. Kriegsbilder, photo album
Autograph album. Assembled by the crew of HMS Illustrious with an original drawing by William Heath Robinson
Autograph album. First World War Commanders and 29 others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signatures include Winston Churchill, Ernst Shackleton and various military commanders.

did.unittitle: Autograph album. First World War Generals including Field Marshal The Earl Haig
Fliegerbilder aus dem Krieg, German photo-reconnaissance of the Somme region from various Flieger Abteilungs (Flier Detachments), photo album
"Germans on the Western Front", photograph collection
Harvey, (Captain) Henry. Photo album of the Western Front.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes twelve photographs of various German aircraft, including an in situ photograph of the wreckage of the Red Baron's (Manfred Albrecht Freiherr von Richthofen) last Fokker Dr.I triplane, serial number 425/17.

did.unittitle: Harvey, (Lieutenant) W.F.J., MC. DCM. Personal Autograph Album of the WWI RFC Ace.
Jessel, (Colonel) Sir Herbert. An Album of material relating to Colonel Sir Herbert Jessel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typed and handwritten letters, photographs, invitations, military passes, memorial cards, newspaper cuttings and other material.

did.unittitle: Heaton, (Nurse) Annie. A personalised autograph book, inscribed by 165 wounded allied soldiers with comments, poems, and sketches
Meeks, (Private) Ernest. Postcard album.
Pease, Cyril. Correspondence, photographs, drawings and ephemera relating to Ambulance Driver Cyril Pease (Red Cross and RNSA)
Ritch, A.H. Photo postcard album from A H Ritch 54 Batt AIF to F. Bryant Queens R. W Surrey in remembrance of days spent in captivity in Erfurt, Germany
Tooley, (Sergeant) Thomas Edward. Photograph and postcard collection
Wright, J.S. Postcards and souvenir photographs of the great air raids on England

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains postcards from J.S. Wright to his wife, as well as a booklet regarding the 1916 Zeppelin raids on Southern England.

did.unittitle: Maps, publications, and other material

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by author or title.

did.unittitle: Allan, M.F. Princess Mary Christmas 1914 Brass Gift Box

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a Christmas Card, unopened pipe tobacco and cigarettes, and a "Silver Bullet" pencil.

did.unittitle: Fischer, (Generalmajor) Eberhard. Lesen des Luftbildes
The Sarajevo Trial documents and State Attorney's appeal against the verdict
Union Jack Club Calendar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an invitation to the twenty-fifth Anniversary of the Marrage of King George V and Queen Mary, July 6 1918. Signed by W.R. Inge, Sandhurst.

did.unittitle: Zeppelin L 15 cloth fragment

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commanded by Kapitänleutnant Joachim Breithaupt, Zeppelin L15 received a direct hit from the anti-aircraft gun at Purfleet, Essex on the 31st March 1916. Attacked again by Second Lieutenant Alfred de Bathe Brandon RFC, 19 RA Sqn (Hainault Farm), in a BE 2C, the air ship eventually became too heavy and crashed in the sea off Margate at 00.15 on 1 April 1916.
